what_it_does: This noticeboard item proposes and documents a new board item type called annotation, giving it a flat-opaque id, an addressable board:// URI, a node_type drawn from a scale/level registry, and a mode lens that distinguishes content from UI.
observations: Frontmatter declares id item-fbc9adde, address board://item-fbc9adde, type block, source claude_code, and state current.
The title begins with P7 and is truncated to lifecyc in the frontmatter.
It links via part_of to board://item-e42d651f.
Channel is dragnet-development, author_session is ch-3mpkjg3r, and thread is empty.
History shows a single filed transition with identical created and updated timestamps.
The body heading repeats the annotation type proposal.
The body schema lists id pattern item-<hex>, address board://item-<hex>, type annotation, node_type paragraph|element|section|…, and mode content|ui.
It describes the envelope as addressable, replyable, and routable.
It frames node_type as SCALE/LEVEL and mode as a LENS, not a second system.
standouts: Proposes a first-class annotation item type with its own lifecycle.
Separates node_type as structural scale/level from mode as content/UI lens.
Asserts that the envelope is addressable and therefore replyable and routable.
Includes a part_of link to another board item.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, thread, links, created, updated, and history.
Uses the board:// URI scheme for item addresses.
Uses a markdown body with a level-1 heading.
Follows the noticeboard item file layout.
concerns: Frontmatter title is truncated (lifecyc) and differs slightly from the body heading.
The promised lifecycle is mentioned in the title but not defined in the body.
The node-type registry and its allowed values are referenced but not present in this file.
The schema block is not fenced as code and mixes YAML-like syntax inside markdown.
notes: A design note that sketches a new annotation board item type and its identity, addressability, and schema, but leaves lifecycle details undefined.
questions: What are the exact stages of the annotation lifecycle?
What entries exist in the node-type registry and who maintains it?
How is mode implemented as a lens rather than a separate system?
What is the content and role of the parent item-e42d651f?
Is the schema block normative or illustrative?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to introduce a new annotation item type with its own lifecycle; the file actually defines identity, addressability, and schema fields but does not spell out lifecycle behavior, so it partially fulfills the stated purpose.
apparent_themes: Separation of structural scale from presentation lens.
Addressability and identity for every envelope.
Avoiding duplicate systems by using lenses.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to add a lightweight, addressable annotation substrate to the noticeboard so that small content or UI nodes can be tracked as first-class items without creating a parallel item hierarchy.
novelty: It treats annotation as a distinct board item type with its own lifecycle, adds a mode field described as a lens rather than a separate subsystem, and separates node_type as a scale/level concept from mode as a view concept.
contribution: It contributes a concrete schema proposal for an annotation item type, including identity rules, addressability claims, and the node_type/mode distinction, which a fused system could retain as a design reference for extending the board item taxonomy.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard item that proposes a new board item type named annotation, specifying id and address patterns, a node_type registry, and a content-or-UI mode lens, while asserting that every envelope is addressable and routable.
intention_for_embedding: To introduce and define the schema and identity model for a new annotation item type in the dragnet-development noticeboard.
